item.title: Italy’s Meloni Says It’s ‘Too Early’ to Tap Russian Gas
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said it was too early to consider tapping Russian gas despite the Iran war driving up energy prices and squeezing supply.

item.title: ASML Shares Get Harder to Value as Firm Stops Disclosing Orders
item.description: ASML Holding NV will omit the one metric that has driven its share price more than any other when it reports earnings Wednesday, injecting uncertainty into a stock nearing record highs.

item.title: Macron, Starmer to Hold Hormuz Transit Summit in Paris on Friday
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will host a videoconference from Paris on Friday with countries willing to help restore free transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: BE Semiconductor Hits Record High as BofA Says Market is Underestimating Demand
item.description: BE Semiconductor Industries NV shares scaled new highs on Tuesday as Bank America Corp. analysts raised their price target to a level above all others, saying strong demand for the Dutch firm’s products is underestimated.

item.title: Veolia Sees €1 Billion in Revenue From AI Industries by 2030
item.description: France’s Veolia Environnement SA plans to more than double revenue from artificial intelligence industries to €1 billion ($1.2 billion) by 2030 as it provides new services to the booming sector.

item.title: Turkey School Shooting Leaves 16 Wounded, Gunman Dead
item.description: An attacker opened fire at a high school in Turkey’s southeast, wounding 16 people before dying by suicide.

item.title: BlackRock Pulls in $130 Billion of Client Cash, Driven by ETFs
item.description: BlackRock Inc. took in a net $130 billion of client cash in the first quarter, with investor money continuing to pour in despite volatility in the public and private markets and protracted uncertainty over the war in Iran.

item.title: Cocoa Price Crash Spurs Hopes for Demand Recovery, Cheaper Candy
item.description: The collapse in cocoa futures is stoking optimism among some industry players that the pace of processing will improve as cheaper beans filter through the supply chain, easing the price of candy bars.

item.title: Poste CEO Says Telecom Italia Is ‘Perfect Fit’ for Digital Hub
item.description: Poste Italiane SpA decided to buy full ownership of Telecom Italia SpA to capture all the potential benefits of working with the former phone monopoly, Chief Executive Officer Matteo Del Fante said.

item.title: China’s Yancoal Buys Australia Mine Stake for Up to $2.4 Billion
item.description: Chinese-controlled Yancoal Australia Ltd. agreed to acquire an 80% stake in the Kestrel coking coal mine in Queensland for as much as $2.4 billion.

item.title: Spain Business Confidence Falls 2.3% in 2Q, Most Since 4Q 2022
item.description: Spain’s business confidence fell 2.3% in the second quarter compared with the first quarter, the biggest decline since the end of 2022, according to data from the Spanish statistics office. The survey showed 21% of business establishments expect a favorable quarter, while 16% are pessimistic about their business outlook.

item.title: UK’s Intertek Mulls Breakup to Lift Product Testing Firm’s Value
item.description: Intertek Group Plc shares surged after the product testing firm said it’s exploring a breakup aimed at boosting its value.

item.title: Malaysia’s Fuel Subsidy Bill Swells to $1.8 Billion in April
item.description: Malaysia’s government expects to spend about 7 billion ringgit ($1.8 billion) on fuel subsidies in April, roughly ten times more than what it was paying before the Iran war.

item.title: Stock Investors Look Past Iran War Despite Shaky Ceasefire
item.description: S&P 500 has erased all its losses tied to the conflict, even as oil and interest rates remain elevated.

item.title: Golden Goose Starts Marketing Bond Sale Linked to PE Firm Buyout
item.description: Golden Goose Group SpA has started marketing a €880 million ($1.04 billion) bond sale to fund its acquisition by Chinese private equity firm HSG, a test of investor sentiment given struggles in the luxury sector and concerns around the Iran war.

item.title: Vietnam Ready to Act to Stabilize Dong, Will Boost Liquidity
item.description: Vietnam’s central bank said it’s prepared to intervene to ensure stability in the foreign exchange market, vowing to also contain inflation and improve liquidity issues facing the banking system.

item.title: BOJ Is Said to Mull Raising Price Outlook Sharply on Higher Oil
item.description: Bank of Japan officials are likely to consider raising their inflation forecast sharply at their policy meeting this month, mainly to reflect elevated oil prices, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chinese Tire Venture Service Long Files for Record Pakistan IPO
item.description: Service Long March Tyres Ltd., Pakistan’s largest manufacturer of all-steel radial tires, has filed for an initial public offering next month that could be the largest private-sector listing in the nation’s history.

item.title: Treasuries Climb as Oil Prices Drop on Iran Peace Deal Optimism
item.description: Treasuries advanced and oil prices retreated, fueled by optimism that progress is being made toward a US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Private Jet Company Bond Ups Bombardier Commitment to $5 Billion
item.description: Private jet company Bond is expanding its commitment with Bombardier Inc. to about $5 billion, betting ultra-wealthy travelers will pay a premium for a club-style experience in the sky.

item.title: Instacart Buys Grocery Tech Firm Instaleap in International Push
item.description: Instacart has acquired the Colombian grocery technology firm Instaleap, part of a broader effort to expand its business outside the US.

item.title: Japanese Bathtub Makers Halt Orders as Material Shortages Bite
item.description: Major Japanese home equipment supplier Cleanup Corp. has suspended orders for its popular bathroom units, the latest in a string of companies suffering material shortages as the Iran conflict stretches on.

item.title: Korea’s Pension Fund NPS Expands Currency Hedging to Bolster Won
item.description: South Korea’s National Pension Service, one of the world’s largest pension funds, is revising its rules to permit greater foreign‑exchange hedging as it seeks to ease pressure on the won.

item.title: IEA Says Gulf Can Resume Half of Shut Oil Fields Weeks After War
item.description: Top Arab Gulf oil producers can return half of shut fields to prewar levels of production within two weeks once transits through the Strait of Hormuz resumes, the International Energy Agency said.

item.title: German Solar Boom Saves Nine LNG Cargoes to Ease Iran War Shock
item.description: German solar power generation is set to surge this summer, helping to shield Europe from some Iran war fallout as it curbs demand for liquefied natural gas imports.

item.title: ING Plans SRT Tied to Project Finance, Including for Oil and Gas
item.description: ING Groep NV is planning a significant risk transfer on €3.5 billion ($4.1 billion) of loans to projects in sectors including oil, gas and renewable power as the Netherlands’ largest lender steps up its use of such instruments.

item.title: Equinor Halves Scatec Stake as Focus Grows on Oil, Gas Output
item.description: Equinor ASA halved its stake in renewables developer Scatec ASA in a further realignment toward its core hydrocarbon business.

item.title: Switzerland Says It’s Ready to Help in Talks to End Iran War
item.description: Switzerland said it’s ready to provide diplomatic support to efforts to end the Iran war amid speculation that the country could host talks between Tehran and Washington in Geneva.

item.title: Drone Hits on Russia Ports Put Indian Refining at Risk, IEA Says
item.description: Ukraine’s drone strikes on key Russian oil-export outlets in the Baltic and Black seas may put India’s crude-refining operations at risk in the near future, according to the International Energy Agency.

item.title: ECB’s Rehn Says Inflation to Quicken But Rate Path Not Locked In
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Olli Rehn said the Iran war will drive inflation higher this year but policymakers can’t commit to a path for borrowing costs.
